dungeon.note: Outputs byte-identical (JSON deep-equal) across 90 seed/size/level combinations before timing. Hand-written port inlines grid indexing and uses function declarations; algorithm and LCG identical.

whereGuard.note: Two constrained params (type Pos = int where value >= 0) on a trivial add. Measured as median of 7 alternating rounds of 3M direct calls each. Guard = one predicate call + branch per constrained param; overhead % is worst-case since the fn body is a single add.

memo.vsNoMemoNote: Same body without @memo takes 206.38 ms per call every time; @memo pays 210.86 ms once then ~0.4 µs per repeat call. Cache is a Map keyed by JSON.stringify(args), unbounded.

codeSize.note: Non-empty lines. Axon generated JS carries a fixed stdlib + test-runner prelude (~334 lines) in every emitted file plus JSDoc for each fn.

findings: Dungeon generator: Axon-generated JS runs at 1.09x the time of hand-written JS (outputs verified identical across 90 seed/size/level cases).
where-guard: constraint validation adds ~-0.1 ns per call (-2% on a trivial add; two guarded params). Guard correctly throws on violation: true.
@memo: caching is real — repeat call with same arg took 0.4 µs vs 210.86 ms cold (562,298x). Cache key is JSON.stringify of args, so keying costs scale with arg size.

Code size: controls.axn is 371 src lines vs 249 for the TS twin (1.49x). Axon's generated JS is 741 lines / 29060 bytes vs tsc's 238 lines / 13035 bytes (2.2x bytes); 334 lines of that is a fixed per-file stdlib/test-runner prelude.
